$comment: Phase 2.2 authoring source — new UCs and CIM Models backfills for mini-categories 22.35-22.49. Consumed by scripts/generate_phase2_mini_categories.py. JSON-first authoring artefact: do not hand-edit the rendered markdown or sidecars; edit this file and re-run the generator.

new_ucs.title: Log signing chain integrity — cryptographic signature drift on evidence archive
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT validate the authenticity of the originating source system; only the unbroken signature chain once evidence reaches the archive.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'log_signature_chain_verification' (hourly) outputs signature-chain breaks with block-number and expected-vs-actual hash to the compliance summary index.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Detects breaks in the integrity component of the trail; confidentiality and availability components need other controls.
Integrity controls for ePHI are directly implemented by an unbroken hash-chain on the evidence archive.
PCI 10.3.4 'file integrity monitoring or change-detection mechanisms on audit logs' is satisfied by a cryptographic-signature chain that emits failures.
A broken signature chain is a textbook ITGC deficiency but the control also needs management attestation.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A deliberate rewrite of an archive block (test fixture) causes a hash mismatch versus the parent block's digest, and the UC fires within the next hourly interval naming the specific block and hash pair.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Normal append-only archive writes with correctly linked hashes do NOT fire.

new_ucs.dataSources: Evidence archive signature-verification events (e.g., sourcetype=archive:signer emitted by the signer service), WORM repository state REST API, KMS signing audit logs.
new_ucs.spl:
index=compliance sourcetype=archive:signer earliest=-1h
| eval block_parent_hash=coalesce(parent_hash,"GENESIS")
| stats values(block_hash) as block_hashes latest(expected_parent_hash) as expected_parent by block_id archive_id
| where expected_parent!=block_parent_hash AND block_parent_hash!="GENESIS"
| table archive_id block_id block_parent_hash expected_parent block_hashes
new_ucs.description: Continuously verifies that every new block in the write-once evidence archive references the immediately-preceding block by cryptographic hash. A mismatch is definitive evidence of either a faulty archive writer or a deliberate rewrite — either way a finding that regulators treat as a break in the chain of custody.
new_ucs.value: Replaces the quarterly 'we sampled 10 logs and checked hashes' audit procedure with a continuous control whose output is either 'zero breaks for the period' or a named remediation record. This is the evidence regulators actually ask for.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ensure the archive signer emits a per-block event with block_id, block_hash, parent_hash, and expected_parent_hash; (2) schedule the UC hourly; (3) route hits to compliance_summary; (4) SOAR auto-opens a P1 with the relevant archive owner; (5) run an automated rebuild drill monthly to ensure verification code itself is not silently broken.
new_ucs.visualization: Timeline of verified vs broken blocks, scatter of block_id × hash-mismatch severity, single-value '% of archive blocks verified (30d)'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Archive signer upgrades that rotate the hash function produce a one-off legitimate 'parent-missing' at the rotation boundary — record the rotation in archive_rotations.csv and allow-list by timestamp.

new_ucs.title: Search-head audit-trail completeness — deleted or rewritten search jobs
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT detect modifications to events at the indexer layer (covered by 22.35.2); only tampering with the search-head audit trail describing who ran what.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'search_audit_tamper' (every 15 min) outputs any _audit events whose artifact_id has subsequently disappeared from dispatch/, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Audit controls must capture search activity on ePHI; detecting deletion of the activity trail is one of several required aspects.
PCI 10.3.2 'protect audit log files from modification' is directly supported by this UC.
CC7.2 requires anomaly detection over system components; tampering with the activity trail is a specific instance.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An operator with the correct capability deletes a dispatch job folder; the _audit event survives but the artifact is gone — the UC fires within the next 15-minute interval.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Normal dispatch TTL expiry (configured window) cleans dispatch folders without producing a finding because the _audit event is beyond the retention window.

new_ucs.dataSources: _audit (action=search), /services/search/jobs REST for current artifacts, dispatch directory state via Splunk REST.
new_ucs.spl:
index=_audit action=search info=completed earliest=-24h
| stats values(artifact_id) as artifact_ids earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen by search_id user
| mvexpand artifact_ids
| join type=outer artifact_ids [| rest /services/search/jobs splunk_server=* | fields sid dispatchState | rename sid as artifact_ids]
| where isnull(dispatchState) AND (now()-last_seen)<86400
| table user search_id artifact_ids first_seen last_seen
new_ucs.description: Reconciles search-head _audit events against the current dispatch artifact state to detect search jobs that were recorded as completed but whose artifacts have since been removed outside the normal dispatch TTL. Mass deletion of dispatch artefacts is a classic post-incident tampering pattern.
new_ucs.value: Gives the Splunk admin team a defensible 'every search run is accounted for' control rather than the weaker 'we have search logging enabled'.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ensure _audit is retained for ≥90 days on the evidence cluster; (2) adjust dispatch_ttl per risk tier; (3) schedule every 15 min; (4) on hit, SOAR opens a P2 for the Splunk admin team.
new_ucs.visualization: Time chart of job-vs-artifact reconciliation, table of missing artifacts by user, single-value 'search jobs with artifact present (7d)'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1070.006 Indicator Removal: Timestomp

new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Cluster re-balancing or search-head restart can transiently remove dispatch artifacts before the next sync; tolerate with a 30-minute post-restart grace via maintenance_windows.csv.

new_ucs.title: DSAR identity-verification friction — failed-verification anomaly
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT audit the DSAR fulfillment clock (covered by 22.36.1); focuses on upstream identity verification that can legitimately or illegitimately slow fulfillment.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'dsar_verification_friction' (daily) identifies subjects with ≥3 failed verifications in a 14-day window and reports to the DPO review queue.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.12(6) allows controllers to ask for identity confirmation but repeated failures can indicate a design that frustrates rights — the UC surfaces the pattern.
CCPA requires a verifiable consumer request process; excessive friction is an enforcement pattern.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A single subject_token with four failed identity verifications in 14 days appears in the output the next day.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A new-ticket flow with one correctly-matched verification does not appear.

new_ucs.dataSources: DSAR platform identity-verification events (OneTrust, TrustArc, ServiceNow Privacy), IdP step-up audit logs, manual review tickets.
new_ucs.spl:
index=dsar sourcetype=onetrust:dsar OR sourcetype=servicenow:privacy action=verification earliest=-14d
| eval pass=if(result="success",1,0), fail=if(result="failure",1,0)
| stats sum(pass) as passes sum(fail) as failures values(ticket_id) as ticket_ids by subject_token
| where failures>=3 AND passes<1
| table subject_token failures ticket_ids
new_ucs.description: Surfaces data-subjects whose right-exercise attempts repeatedly fail verification, which — aggregated over time — is a reliable indicator either of a broken verification UX or of a deliberate design that frustrates rights. Either interpretation is a DPO priority.
new_ucs.value: Takes the single most common enforcement narrative ('the verification process itself denied the right') and turns it into a measurable metric.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ensure DSAR verification events emit subject_token and result; (2) run daily; (3) feed findings to a DPO review dashboard; (4) after a fixed threshold, escalate via SOAR for legal review.
new_ucs.visualization: Bar chart of verification failure rate per channel, table of repeat-failure subjects, single-value 'repeat-failure subjects (14d)'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Genuine impersonation attempts will (correctly) produce multiple failures for the same subject_token; pair with IdP risk signals before escalating.

new_ucs.title: DSAR request-type mix anomaly — zero-deletion skew indicating broken workflow
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the correctness of an individual DSAR outcome; only the statistical mix across the programme.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'dsar_request_type_mix' (weekly) compares the current-week request-type distribution against a rolling 12-week baseline and alerts on >3σ drift.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A month with zero erasures across a 10,000-subject controller is a statistical anomaly worth investigating; this UC raises the signal.
CCPA reporting metrics require accurate categorisation; a sudden request-type skew often means a broken form.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A code change accidentally routes all 'delete my data' buttons to the access-request endpoint; in the following week the UC fires with a 99% access / 1% deletion mix (baseline 60/40).
controlTest.negativeScenario: Normal week-over-week fluctuation in request-type mix does NOT fire.

new_ucs.dataSources: DSAR ticketing system request-type field, historic aggregates from compliance summary index.
new_ucs.spl:
index=dsar sourcetype=onetrust:dsar OR sourcetype=servicenow:privacy earliest=-84d
| bin _time span=7d
| stats count by _time request_type
| eventstats sum(count) as total by _time
| eval ratio=count/total
| stats avg(ratio) as baseline, stdev(ratio) as baseline_sd latest(ratio) as current by request_type
| eval drift=abs(current-baseline)
| where drift>(3*baseline_sd) AND baseline_sd>0
| table request_type baseline current drift baseline_sd
new_ucs.description: Detects statistically anomalous drift in the distribution of DSAR request types (access / rectification / erasure / portability / opt-out). A sudden zero-erasure week across a large estate is usually a symptom of a broken UX flow rather than genuine user choice.
new_ucs.value: Makes the common 'silent bug in the privacy portal' failure mode visible without waiting for a complaint.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ensure DSAR events emit a canonical request_type from an allow-list; (2) run weekly; (3) on fire, open a DPO + engineering review ticket.
new_ucs.visualization: Stacked area of request-type mix over 12 weeks, bar chart of current-vs-baseline deltas, single-value 'drift categories this week'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Seasonal campaigns (e.g., year-end 'delete my data' reminders) legitimately shift the mix; maintain a known-campaign lookup to dampen the signal for those windows.

new_ucs.title: Purpose-limitation enforcement — processing not matching declared purpose
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT establish lawful basis itself; surfaces processing-vs-purpose mismatches for DPO review.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'purpose_limitation_audit' (hourly) emits events where declared purpose in the access token disagrees with the processing system's declared purpose.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.5(1)(b) 'purpose limitation' requires technical enforcement wherever feasible; this UC detects observable mismatches.
CPRA §1798.100(b) requires use limitation to disclosed purposes; a mismatch is direct evidence of a deficiency.
LGPD Art.6(I) 'finalidade' mirrors purpose limitation; same surveillance value.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A marketing job fetches data with token-purpose=personalization but reaches a system whose declared purpose is fraud-prevention only; the UC fires within the hour.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Token-purpose and system-purpose align; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: Application audit logs carrying the purpose-bearing access token, system-purpose catalogue in purpose_catalogue.csv.
new_ucs.spl:
index=app_activity earliest=-1h token_purpose=*
| lookup purpose_catalogue.csv system_id OUTPUT allowed_purposes
| eval ok=if(match(allowed_purposes,token_purpose),1,0)
| where ok=0
| stats count values(user) as users values(subject_token) as subjects by system_id token_purpose allowed_purposes
| table system_id token_purpose allowed_purposes count users subjects
new_ucs.description: Observes access tokens carrying a declared processing purpose and compares that purpose to the declared purposes of the system being accessed. Any mismatch is evidence that the purpose-limitation principle is not being enforced.
new_ucs.value: Converts an otherwise-abstract regulatory principle into a continuously-measurable control with evidentiary value.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Mint OAuth/OIDC tokens with a purpose claim; (2) publish purpose_catalogue.csv keyed by system_id; (3) run hourly; (4) alert DPO on mismatches and drive remediation via SOAR.
new_ucs.visualization: Sankey of token-purpose → system, heatmap of mismatches by system, single-value 'mismatch events (24h)'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Shared service accounts without per-request purpose context cannot be evaluated; filter them out via service_account_allowlist.csv.

new_ucs.title: IAB TCF consent string mutation without user interaction
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the legality of the initial consent; focuses on downstream mutation without an originating user interaction event.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'tcf_consent_mutation' (hourly) emits sessions whose TCF v2.2 consent string changed between page impressions without a corresponding CMP interaction event.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.7(1) requires the controller to demonstrate consent; detecting silent string mutation is one part of that demonstration.
CPRA opt-out signals (GPC) can be misrepresented by server-side mutation; detection adds evidentiary weight.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An ad tag overwrites the TC string for a session without a CMP interaction event in the same window; the UC fires and names the tag and vendor.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A legitimate CMP interaction (user clicks 'Save preferences') legally changes the TC string; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: Web access logs capturing Set-Cookie: euconsent-v2, CMP interaction events (onetrust:cmp), client-side analytics.
new_ucs.spl:
index=web sourcetype=access_combined earliest=-1h euconsent_v2=*
| sort 0 session_id _time
| streamstats current=f last(euconsent_v2) as prior_consent by session_id
| where isnotnull(prior_consent) AND euconsent_v2!=prior_consent
| join type=outer session_id _time [search index=cmp sourcetype=onetrust:cmp action=interaction earliest=-1h | fields session_id _time | rename _time as interaction_time]
| where isnull(interaction_time) OR abs(_time-interaction_time)>60
| table _time session_id prior_consent euconsent_v2
new_ucs.description: Compares the TCF 2.2 consent string across consecutive page impressions in the same session and flags changes that occurred without a corresponding CMP interaction within 60 seconds. This is a known pattern of rogue ad-tech overriding user choice.
new_ucs.value: Catches a pattern that has surfaced repeatedly in EDPB enforcement decisions (Meta, Google ad-tech) before it becomes a supervisory finding.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Preserve Set-Cookie headers in access logs; (2) ingest CMP interaction events; (3) run hourly; (4) on hit, freeze the vendor via ad-tech allow-list and open a DPO ticket.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of mutating sessions by vendor, bar chart of mutations per ad-tech partner, single-value 'sessions with genuine consent (24h)'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: CMP token refreshes (same purpose mask, new expiry) can legitimately rewrite the TC string; allow-list based on purpose-bitmap equality.

new_ucs.title: Transfer Impact Assessment currency — stale Schrems II assessments
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the substantive quality of a TIA; surfaces only TIAs that have not been reviewed within policy cadence.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'tia_currency' (weekly) lists TIAs past their review date with the related transfer flows.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.46 SCC safeguards rely on a maintained TIA; detecting staleness is a direct operational control.
UK equivalent to Art.46 is maintained through TIAs (ICO International Data Transfer Agreement).

controlTest.positiveScenario: A TIA last reviewed 15 months ago (policy cadence: 12 months) appears in the next weekly run.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A TIA reviewed within the last 12 months does not appear.

new_ucs.dataSources: TIA register (tia_register.csv), transfer_register.csv, DPO review events.
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup tia_register.csv
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_reviewed,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where age_days>cadence_days
| lookup transfer_register.csv tia_id OUTPUT transfer_id destination
| table tia_id destination last_reviewed age_days cadence_days transfer_id
new_ucs.description: Makes TIA currency a visible metric rather than a yearly memory item. Stale TIAs are the most common Schrems II finding by supervisory authorities.
new_ucs.value: Converts a calendar reminder into a demonstrable control with evidence per-transfer.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain tia_register.csv; (2) run weekly; (3) on miss, DPO re-opens the TIA review workflow.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of stale TIAs with remediation owner, bar chart of TIAs by age bucket, single-value 'TIAs within cadence'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: TIAs under active review (with a workflow_state='in_review' flag) should be excluded via workflow_state.

new_ucs.title: Bulk regulated-data export targeting non-adequate jurisdiction
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate small single-record flows (covered by 22.38.1); targets bulk operations ≥1GB or ≥10k records.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'bulk_export_cross_border' (every 10 min) outputs bulk export operations whose destination geography lacks adequacy or SCC cover.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.44 general principle for transfers is directly enforced by blocking bulk exports without safeguards.
Art.45 adequacy decision list membership is directly queried.
DORA Art.28 ICT third-party oversight implicates cross-border data dependencies.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A SQL dump of 250k customer records is written to an S3 bucket in a non-adequate jurisdiction; the UC fires within 10 min.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A bulk export to an adequacy-listed or SCC-covered jurisdiction does NOT fire.

new_ucs.dataSources: DB export audit logs, cloud storage PUT events, DLP bulk-operation classifications, sanctioned_transfer_jurisdictions.csv.
new_ucs.spl:
index=db_audit action=export OR (index=cloud_storage action=PutObject bytes>1073741824) earliest=-10m
| lookup data_classification.csv object_key OUTPUT classification regulated_scope
| where isnotnull(regulated_scope)
| `get_geolocation(dest_ip)`
| lookup sanctioned_transfer_jurisdictions.csv country as dest_country OUTPUT scc_active adequacy_decision
| where isnull(scc_active) AND isnull(adequacy_decision)
| table _time user object_key classification regulated_scope dest_country bytes
new_ucs.description: Intersects bulk export operations (DB dumps, large cloud uploads) with destination geolocation and the sanctioned-transfer catalogue. Bulk exports targeting non-adequate jurisdictions without SCCs are a category of breach that requires immediate executive escalation.
new_ucs.value: Turns post-hoc 'where did our customer dataset go?' incident forensics into real-time prevention.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ensure DB and cloud storage layers emit size + destination; (2) maintain classification and jurisdiction lookups; (3) schedule every 10 min; (4) SOAR auto-blocks destination if exception policy permits.
new_ucs.visualization: World-map of bulk exports, table of findings with destination geography, single-value 'bulk exports within sanctioned geographies'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service

new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Bona-fide DR or migration operations authorised under a dedicated exception ID must be logged to a migrations.csv allow-list.

new_ucs.title: Cross-regulator consistency — divergent material facts across submissions
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate submission timeliness (covered by 22.39.1); focuses on factual consistency across parallel submissions.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'regulator_submission_divergence' (post-submission) compares affected-subject and data-class counts across submissions sharing the same incident_id.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.33(3) requires specific material facts; divergence across submissions can be challenged.
NIS2 requires a final report with same material facts; inconsistency is a deficiency.
DORA Art.19 initial and final reports must be internally consistent.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Two submissions for incident_id=INC-123 report 1,000 and 10,000 affected subjects respectively; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Submissions with aligned subject counts do not fire.

new_ucs.dataSources: regulator:submission sourcetype records with normalised material-fact fields (affected_subjects, data_classes, root_cause).
new_ucs.spl:
index=notifications sourcetype=regulator:submission earliest=-30d
| stats values(affected_subjects) as subjects_set values(data_classes) as data_set dc(affected_subjects) as subjects_uniques dc(data_classes) as data_uniques by incident_id
| where subjects_uniques>1 OR data_uniques>1
| table incident_id subjects_set data_set
new_ucs.description: Reads the structured material-fact fields of every regulator submission for an incident and alerts when the same incident has been described with divergent numbers or data classes. A diverging narrative across regulators is a pattern that escalates enforcement severity.
new_ucs.value: Prevents the 'regulator cross-reference' problem that has escalated several prominent supervisory cases from technical fines to broader corporate sanction.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Normalise submission events to carry canonical material-fact fields; (2) run 24h after any new submission for the incident_id; (3) alert DPO + legal if divergence found.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of divergent incidents, bar chart of divergence magnitude, single-value 'incidents with consistent submissions'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Genuine refinement of scope between the initial 72h notice and the 30-day follow-up is legitimate and expected; parametrise a tolerance window.

new_ucs.title: Regulator-portal authentication failure during submission window
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the content of the submission; focuses on the authentication path that precedes it.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'regulator_portal_mfa_failure' (real-time) alerts when an automation account fails MFA on a regulator portal inside an open-incident window.

compliance.assurance_rationale: An aborted notification-flow due to MFA failure is a root cause of missed SLAs; this UC prevents silent failure.
Same for NIS2 initial notification obligation.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An automation account used for regulator submissions fails MFA during an open-incident window; UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Same account authenticates successfully; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: IdP audit logs (okta:audit, azuread:audit), incident_register.csv with open_status flag.
new_ucs.spl:
index=idp sourcetype=okta:audit OR sourcetype=azuread:audit earliest=-15m result=failure
| search user IN (`regulator_service_accounts`)
| join user [| inputlookup incident_register.csv | where status="open" | fields user=regulator_user incident_id]
| table _time user incident_id src_ip failure_reason
new_ucs.description: Joins IdP authentication failure events for a named set of regulator-portal service accounts against the currently open-incident register. An MFA failure in this window is a blocking event for notification SLA compliance.
new_ucs.value: Converts a class of silent failures (an expired portal API key, a stale MFA token) that has materialised in past incidents into an always-on monitoring pattern.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain a named macro regulator_service_accounts listing the IdP principals; (2) run every 5-10 min; (3) SOAR pages the DPO on hit.
new_ucs.visualization: Time chart of portal authentications by account, table of failures, single-value 'successful authentications in open windows'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Portal-initiated credential rotation can cause a single expected failure; require ≥2 failures in 5 min.

new_ucs.title: Standing-privilege credential vaulting drift — admin accounts outside PAM
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate usage of credentials (covered by 22.40.1); focuses on credentials sitting outside the vault entirely.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'pam_vaulting_drift' (daily) cross-references IdP privileged groups against PAM vault inventory and reports accounts not present in the vault.

compliance.assurance_rationale: AC-5 separation of duties and privileged-account vaulting are directly enforced.
PCI 7.2.5.1 requires tight controls on privileged account lifecycle; unvaulted admins fail this control.
Unvaulted privileged credentials are a textbook SOX-ITGC material weakness.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A domain-admin account exists in AzureAD but has no matching record in the CyberArk vault; the UC fires the next day.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All IdP admin accounts are present in the vault; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: IdP privileged-group membership (Azure AD, Okta, LDAP), PAM vault inventory (CyberArk, BeyondTrust, Delinea).
new_ucs.spl:
| rest /services/identity/privileged-groups splunk_server=*
| fields user group
| join type=outer user [| rest /services/pam/vault-inventory splunk_server=* | fields user vault_id]
| where isnull(vault_id)
| stats values(group) as groups by user
| table user groups
new_ucs.description: Treats the PAM vault as the authoritative register of privileged credentials and alerts on any IdP-level admin that is not present in the vault. The asymmetry between 'privileged' and 'managed' is the most common drift pattern.
new_ucs.value: Replaces the quarterly 'access-review export + spreadsheet join' with a daily evidence feed.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Expose IdP privileged groups and PAM vault inventory via REST; (2) run daily; (3) on miss, auto-open a PAM onboarding ticket with the CISO.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of drift accounts by group, bar chart of drift count by tenant, single-value '% of admins vaulted'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1078.003 Valid Accounts: Local Accounts

new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Break-glass accounts are intentionally outside the vault; maintain breakglass_accounts.csv to allow-list.

new_ucs.title: High-risk privileged-session command without JIT approval
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate routine admin workload; fires only when a command matches the high-risk pattern catalogue.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'pam_high_risk_command_jit' (every 5 min) compares command execution against the high-risk catalogue and the JIT approval ledger.

compliance.assurance_rationale: AC-6(9) auditing use of privileged functions is directly enforced; high-risk-command correlation provides evidence.
PCI 10.2.2 requires auditing of individual privileged user actions.
SOX-ITGC requires prevention or evidence of privileged changes; this UC provides evidence for unapproved JIT.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A DBA issues `DROP TABLE` in production without a JIT approval ticket; the UC fires within 5 min.
controlTest.negativeScenario: The same command is issued with a valid JIT approval ticket linked to the session; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: PAM session keystroke/command logs, GRC JIT ticketing events, high-risk command catalogue (high_risk_commands.csv).
new_ucs.spl:
index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:command OR sourcetype=beyondtrust:command earliest=-5m
| lookup high_risk_commands.csv command_pattern AS command OUTPUT risk_class
| where risk_class IN ("destructive","mass-data","security-control")
| join session_id [search index=grc sourcetype=jit:approval earliest=-1h | fields session_id ticket_id approver approved_at]
| where isnull(ticket_id)
| table _time session_id user command risk_class target_host
new_ucs.description: Correlates privileged-session command execution against a catalogue of destructive, mass-data, or security-control commands and requires a matching JIT approval within the prior hour. Missing approvals are treated as a finding.
new_ucs.value: Makes 'we require JIT approval for dangerous commands' an observable, always-on control.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Curate high_risk_commands.csv with regex patterns; (2) instrument GRC JIT to emit session_id; (3) run every 5 min; (4) SOAR auto-terminates session on hit.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of unapproved high-risk commands, bar chart of commands by risk class, single-value 'high-risk commands approved (7d)'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1078.004 Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts

new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Automated runbook executions need a runbook_id that looks like a JIT ticket; ensure runbook automation is integrated with the JIT ledger.

new_ucs.title: TLS downgrade / legacy-cipher handshake spike
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT measure certificate-expiry posture (covered by 22.41.2); focuses on observed handshake protocol/cipher.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'tls_downgrade_spike' (hourly) emits a per-endpoint handshake profile and alerts on legacy-cipher spikes.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 4.2.1 strong cryptography in transmission is directly measured.
Transmission security is required for ePHI.
SC-8 transmission confidentiality; legacy cipher negotiation is a direct failure.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An external load-balancer begins negotiating TLS 1.0 with 5% of clients after a config push; the UC fires within the hour.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All handshakes remain TLS 1.2/1.3 with strong ciphers; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: F5/NGINX/HAProxy TLS handshake logs, cloud-provider LB telemetry, TLS scanner longitudinal results.
new_ucs.spl:
index=tls sourcetype=f5:tls OR sourcetype=nginx:tls OR sourcetype=haproxy:tls earliest=-1h
| eval legacy=if(match(protocol,"TLSv1\.(0|1)") OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT"),1,0)
| stats sum(legacy) as legacy_hs count as total_hs by host
| eval legacy_pct=round(legacy_hs/total_hs*100,2)
| where legacy_pct>0.5 AND total_hs>100
| table host legacy_hs total_hs legacy_pct
new_ucs.description: Monitors observed TLS handshake metadata for any endpoint and alerts when legacy-cipher or legacy-protocol negotiations exceed a small threshold. Downgrade is a common attacker technique against partially-upgraded infrastructure.
new_ucs.value: Catches silent downgrade issues introduced by load-balancer config drift before they become an audit finding or data-exposure incident.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ensure LBs emit handshake telemetry; (2) schedule hourly; (3) route hits to security ops; (4) block via config-as-code rollback.
new_ucs.visualization: Line chart of legacy-handshake percentage per endpoint, table of offending endpoints, single-value '% handshakes on strong TLS (24h)'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1562.006 Impair Defenses: Indicator Blocking

new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Legacy client populations (POS devices) can inflate TLS 1.0 usage in scope of a documented risk-acceptance; allow-list such endpoints.

new_ucs.title: Key custodian SoD — same identity creates AND approves a key
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT audit key usage (covered by 22.41.3); targets SoD on creation/approval.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'kms_key_sod' (every 30 min) emits key lifecycle events where creator == approver.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 3.6.1 key-management role separation is directly enforced.
SC-12 key establishment and management requires separation of duties.
Common SoD ITGC deficiency around encryption-key management.

controlTest.positiveScenario: User alice creates a key and then approves its activation in the same audit trail; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: alice creates and bob approves; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: KMS audit logs (aws:kms, azure:keyvault, gcp:kms), HSM audit logs.
new_ucs.spl:
index=kms earliest=-30m (action=CreateKey OR action=ScheduleKeyActivation)
| stats values(user) as users values(action) as actions by key_id
| eval distinct_users=mvcount(mvdedup(users))
| where distinct_users<2 AND mvcount(actions)>=2
| table key_id users actions
new_ucs.description: Alerts when the same identity both creates a key and schedules its activation. SoD in key management is a PCI and SOX-ITGC hard requirement.
new_ucs.value: Provides daily evidence of key-custodian SoD rather than the common annual sample.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ingest KMS audit logs; (2) run every 30 min; (3) escalate to the crypto custodian.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of SoD violations, bar chart of violations by key type, single-value 'SoD-compliant key operations (30d)'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Automated key provisioning pipelines that run under a single service account may trigger this UC; they must be structured as two-stage with distinct approver accounts.

new_ucs.title: Change rollback execution evidence — declared rollback vs actual
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate whether the change itself was authorised (covered by 22.42.1); focuses on rollback readiness.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'change_rollback_evidence' (weekly) emits CRs whose declared rollback plan was never exercised during the test window.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CM-3 configuration-change control requires tested rollback.
CC8.1 change-management evidence requires tested rollback.
SOX ITGC rollback evidence is satisfied in a risk-based manner.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A CR claims a rollback plan exists but the rollback pipeline was never invoked in its 6-month test cadence; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A CR whose rollback was exercised (pipeline_event=rollback_test success) does NOT fire.

new_ucs.dataSources: ITSM CR records (ServiceNow), CI/CD pipeline audit (rollback_test events).
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup itsm_changes.csv
| where rollback_plan_required="true"
| lookup rollback_tests.csv change_id OUTPUT last_tested
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_tested,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(last_tested) OR age_days>180
| table change_id rollback_plan_required last_tested age_days
new_ucs.description: Continuously verifies that every change with a declared rollback plan has exercised that plan within policy cadence (default 180 days).
new_ucs.value: Turns rollback-plan existence from a documentation artefact into a tested capability.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Export CR metadata to itsm_changes.csv; (2) have CI/CD emit rollback_test events; (3) run weekly; (4) on miss, schedule a rollback rehearsal.
new_ucs.visualization: Aging bar chart of changes without rollback test, table of at-risk CRs, single-value 'CRs with tested rollback'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Emergency hotfix CRs may legitimately have a 'forward-fix-only' rollback strategy; annotate with rollback_type to exclude them.

new_ucs.title: CAB approval bypass — change pushed before scheduled window
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate emergency changes with a retro-CR (handled explicitly via approval_type=emergency).
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'cab_bypass' (hourly) emits pipeline apply events outside the CR scheduled window.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Direct evidence of an unapproved change path.
Direct evidence of an unapproved change path.
SOX ITGC change-approval deficiency pattern.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A deployment pipeline applies a change 2 hours before the CR scheduled window; the UC fires within the hour.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Pipeline apply inside the scheduled window; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: CI/CD pipeline apply events tagged with change_id, ServiceNow CR schedule data.
new_ucs.spl:
index=change sourcetype=prod:apply earliest=-1h
| join change_id [search index=itsm sourcetype=servicenow:change | fields change_id window_start window_end approval_type]
| eval start_s=strptime(window_start,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), end_s=strptime(window_end,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| where approval_type!="emergency" AND (_time<start_s OR _time>end_s)
| table _time change_id applied_by window_start window_end
new_ucs.description: Correlates every production apply event to its CR scheduled window and emits those that landed outside the declared window. Out-of-window changes that are not flagged as emergency are a primary ITGC deficiency.
new_ucs.value: Turns CAB approval from a calendar event into an operational control with per-change evidence.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Force CI/CD pipelines to emit change_id; (2) ingest CR schedule data; (3) run hourly; (4) SOAR auto-freezes the pipeline on hit.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of out-of-window applies, bar chart of offending pipelines, single-value '% of changes applied in window'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1562.001 Impair Defenses: Disable Tools

new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Timezone-normalised window_start/window_end is critical; a TZ mismatch will falsely flag correct changes.

new_ucs.title: Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the validity of the merged plan itself.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'iac_drift' (every 30 min) compares cloud inventory against the last-merged IaC plan checksum and lists divergences.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CM-6 configuration settings require that the applied state match the approved baseline.
CC8.1 includes drift detection as an observable outcome of change management.
A.8.9 configuration management requires monitoring of drift from baselines.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A manual console change alters a security-group rule that was established via Terraform; the next drift scan finds the difference.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All applied changes carry the current plan's checksum; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: Terraform/OpenTofu state snapshots, cloud provider inventory (AWS Config, Azure Resource Graph, GCP Asset Inventory), CI/CD merge events.
new_ucs.spl:
index=iac sourcetype=tf:state_snapshot earliest=-30m
| stats latest(plan_checksum) as expected_checksum by resource_id
| join resource_id [search index=cloud sourcetype=aws:config OR sourcetype=azure:rg OR sourcetype=gcp:asset earliest=-30m | stats latest(state_checksum) as actual_checksum by resource_id]
| where expected_checksum!=actual_checksum
| table resource_id expected_checksum actual_checksum
new_ucs.description: Continuously reconciles the cloud resource inventory against the last-approved IaC plan checksum and alerts on drift. Drift is a canonical change-management deficiency and also a known attacker-persistence pattern.
new_ucs.value: Turns 'we use Terraform' into a continuously-verified statement.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Export per-plan checksums from CI on merge; (2) enable cloud inventory export; (3) run every 30 min; (4) SOAR auto-remediates via `terraform plan && apply` if allowed.
new_ucs.visualization: Heatmap of resources × drift, table of drifting resources, single-value '% of resources matching plan'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1562.001 Impair Defenses: Disable Tools

new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Cloud-provider control-plane metadata changes (timestamps, auto-generated IDs) can fire; exclude from checksum calculation at plan-emission time.

new_ucs.title: Internet-facing asset × unpatched critical CVE
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate internal hosts (covered by 22.43.1 in aggregate).
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'internet_facing_critical_cve' (hourly) intersects external-facing inventory with unpatched critical CVEs present on any such host.

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 risk-management measures include vulnerability handling on exposed assets.
PCI 6.3.3 requires critical vulnerabilities be addressed within one month; Internet-facing is the worst case.
RA-5 vulnerability scanning and remediation; Internet-facing is a priority.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Host web-01 is in the external-facing inventory and has CVE-2024-XXXX (CVSS 9.8) unpatched for 3 days; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Host is on internal network; no alert (different UC handles internal).

new_ucs.dataSources: Vulnerability scanner feeds, external-facing inventory (Shodan, Censys, AWS ALB/NLB, cloud LBs).
new_ucs.spl:
index=vuln earliest=-24h cvss_v3_base>=9.0
| lookup external_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT external_exposed
| where external_exposed="true"
| stats min(_time) as first_seen values(cve) as cves by host
| eval age_days=round((now()-first_seen)/86400,0)
| where age_days>0
| table host cves age_days
new_ucs.description: Any Internet-facing asset with an unpatched critical CVE is the single highest-priority audit and security finding; this UC keeps it at one-hour latency.
new_ucs.value: Eliminates the gap between scanner output and risk-based patch-SLA tracking.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain external_asset_inventory.csv refreshed daily from LB config or external attack-surface tooling; (2) run hourly; (3) SOAR auto-escalates on hit.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of Internet-facing hosts with critical CVEs, bar chart of patch-age buckets, single-value 'Internet-facing hosts fully patched'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application

new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Honeypot / deliberately-exposed research assets can be excluded via an inventory annotation.

new_ucs.title: Scanner coverage gap — regulated hosts without a recent scan
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate scan result quality; focuses on coverage.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'vuln_scanner_coverage_gap' (daily) lists regulated hosts whose most recent scan exceeds the per-tier SLA.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 11.3.1.1 internal vulnerability scans every three months; this UC measures coverage.
RA-5(2) update vulnerability scans on a frequency that is risk-based.
A.8.8 management of technical vulnerabilities includes coverage.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A regulated host has no scan record in the last 35 days (tier-1 SLA: 30 days); the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Regulated host scanned within 30 days; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: Vulnerability scanner inventory, asset_inventory.csv with regulated flag, per-tier SLA lookup.
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup asset_inventory.csv
| where regulated="true"
| lookup scan_history.csv host OUTPUT last_scan
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_scan,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| lookup tier_sla.csv tier OUTPUT scan_sla_days
| where isnull(last_scan) OR days_since>scan_sla_days
| table tier host last_scan days_since scan_sla_days
new_ucs.description: Turns scanner coverage into a continuously-measured KPI instead of a quarterly spreadsheet.
new_ucs.value: Surfaces silent scanner onboarding failures before they become audit findings.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain asset_inventory.csv; (2) export scanner state to scan_history.csv; (3) run daily.
new_ucs.visualization: Heatmap of hosts × days-since-scan, table of offenders, single-value 'regulated coverage'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Hosts mid-onboarding should carry an onboarding_date; allow-list until scan credentials propagate.

new_ucs.title: SBOM vendor-component CVE exposure
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate first-party dependency CVEs (covered by 22.43.1); targets third-party SBOM inputs.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'sbom_cve_exposure' (daily) joins vendor SBOMs against the current CVE feed.

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 supply-chain risk-management clause is supported by SBOM-CVE correlation.
EU Cyber Resilience Act requires product vulnerability management backed by SBOM.
SR-11 component authenticity includes supply-chain vulnerability monitoring.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Vendor component log4j 2.14 is in the SBOM; CVE-2021-44228 is published; the UC fires on the next run.
controlTest.negativeScenario: No vendor component matches the current CVE feed; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: Vendor SBOMs (CycloneDX/SPDX), CVE feed (NVD, vendor advisories).
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup vendor_sbom.csv
| lookup cve_feed.csv component version OUTPUT cve cvss_v3_base
| where isnotnull(cve) AND cvss_v3_base>=7.0
| stats values(cve) as cves, max(cvss_v3_base) as max_cvss by vendor component version
| table vendor component version cves max_cvss
new_ucs.description: Uses the vendor SBOM register to answer 'which vendor components in my estate are affected by the CVE published today?'.
new_ucs.value: Closes the gap between the day a CVE is published and the day a vendor responds — crucial for CRA and NIS2 supply-chain narratives.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ingest vendor SBOMs from the procurement workflow; (2) subscribe to NVD feed into cve_feed.csv; (3) run daily; (4) SOAR opens a case per vendor-affected component.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of exposed components, bar chart of CVE count by vendor, single-value 'SBOM-indexed components with no open CVE'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1195.002 Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain

new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Version ranges expressed in SPDX are sometimes imprecise; maintain a normalisation pre-step to avoid double counting.

new_ucs.title: Vendor access telemetry — principals active outside contracted hours/geos
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT measure vendor-supplied service outages; focuses on vendor principals acting against the estate.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'vendor_access_off_contract' (hourly) emits vendor principals acting outside contracted windows or permitted geographies.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.28 ICT third-party oversight explicitly requires telemetry of third-party access.
NIS2 supply-chain risk-management extends to vendor access.
SR-6 supplier assessments and reviews include telemetry.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Vendor principal vendor_abc@x logs in at 03:00 UTC on Sunday from a geography not on the contract's permitted list; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Same principal during contracted weekday hours from the expected geography; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: IdP authentication events for named vendor principals, vendor contracts lookup with contracted hours/geos.
new_ucs.spl:
index=idp earliest=-1h (sourcetype=okta:audit OR sourcetype=azuread:audit) result=success
| search user IN (`vendor_principals`)
| `get_geolocation(src_ip)`
| lookup vendor_contract.csv user OUTPUT allowed_hours allowed_countries
| eval hour=strftime(_time,"%H"), weekday=strftime(_time,"%A"), window_ok=if(match(allowed_hours,weekday.":".hour),1,0), geo_ok=if(match(allowed_countries,country),1,0)
| where window_ok=0 OR geo_ok=0
| table _time user country hour weekday allowed_countries allowed_hours
new_ucs.description: Intersects vendor principal authentication events with contracted access windows (hours/days) and permitted geographies; divergence is a DORA Art.28 finding.
new_ucs.value: Materialises contractual access controls into operational telemetry.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain vendor_principals macro; (2) extract contracted windows/geos from contracts and publish to vendor_contract.csv; (3) run hourly.
new_ucs.visualization: World map of vendor logins with off-geo markers, bar chart of vendors over time, single-value 'vendor logins within contract (24h)'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1078.004 Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts

new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Planned after-hours maintenance windows need a pre-declared exception with vendor_contract.exception_id.

new_ucs.title: SBOM attestation completeness — critical vendors without signed SBOM
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the content of the SBOM (covered by 22.43.5); focuses on presence/signature.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'sbom_attestation_completeness' (weekly) lists critical vendors without a valid signed SBOM.

compliance.assurance_rationale: EU CRA mandates SBOM provisioning and signature.
NIS2 supply-chain risk management is directly supported.
SR-11(1) authenticity check of components.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A critical vendor has no SBOM in the register; the UC fires weekly until resolved.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A critical vendor has a signed SBOM within policy cadence; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: Vendor SBOM register (vendor_sbom.csv), signature-validation tooling outputs, VRM criticality.
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup vrm_attestations.csv
| where criticality="critical"
| lookup vendor_sbom_attestations.csv vendor OUTPUT sbom_sha256 signed signed_by signed_at
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(signed_at,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(sbom_sha256) OR signed!="true" OR age_days>180
| table vendor criticality sbom_sha256 signed signed_by age_days
new_ucs.description: Surfaces critical vendors whose SBOM is absent, unsigned, or older than policy cadence (default 180 days).
new_ucs.value: Makes the CRA/NIS2 SBOM requirement a tracked operational metric.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Onboard vendor SBOMs through procurement workflow; (2) validate signatures with sigstore or equivalent; (3) run weekly.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of at-risk vendors, bar chart of signature freshness, single-value 'critical vendors with signed SBOM'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Vendors mid-re-signing can appear stale; allow a 7-day grace after signed_attempted_at.

new_ucs.title: Backup repository TLS posture — aged or weak-cipher endpoints
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT validate key rotation (covered by 22.41.3); focuses on transport posture at backup ingress.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'backup_tls_posture' (daily) reports any backup repository endpoint with legacy protocol or near-expiry certificate.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.12 backup policies include protection of transport.
HIPAA transmission security applies to backup ingress.
CP-9(3) protection of backup confidentiality.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Backup repo at backup.example.com negotiates TLS 1.0; the UC fires daily.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All backup endpoints negotiate TLS 1.2/1.3 with strong ciphers; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: TLS scanner output for backup endpoint inventory.
new_ucs.spl:
index=tls sourcetype=tls:scan earliest=-24h
| search host IN (`backup_repository_endpoints`)
| eval days_to_expiry=round((expires-now())/86400,0)
| where days_to_expiry<45 OR protocol="TLSv1.0" OR protocol="TLSv1.1" OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT")
| table host days_to_expiry protocol cipher
new_ucs.description: Ensures the transport to backup repositories — traditionally neglected — matches the same TLS posture as customer-facing systems.
new_ucs.value: Closes a gap pattern observed in DORA inspections where the backup plane had older TLS than the production plane.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Inventory backup endpoints; (2) scan daily; (3) on finding, ticket to platform engineering.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of endpoints with weak posture, time chart of scan results, single-value '% of backup endpoints with strong TLS'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Appliance-managed endpoints with vendor-pinned TLS libraries may need a supply-chain exception.

new_ucs.title: Business-continuity rehearsal evidence — BCP/DR exercise execution logged
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate technical restore SLAs (covered by 22.45.1); focuses on human-orchestrated rehearsal.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'bcp_rehearsal_evidence' (quarterly) emits BUs whose rehearsal is older than the policy cadence.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.24 testing of digital operational resilience, explicitly including rehearsals.
NIS2 business continuity and crisis management.
CP-4 contingency plan testing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A BU has not logged a rehearsal in 13 months (policy cadence: 12 months); the UC fires quarterly.
controlTest.negativeScenario: BU has logged a rehearsal within 12 months; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: BCP rehearsal ticketing system, signed rehearsal attestations.
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup business_units.csv
| lookup bcp_rehearsals.csv bu_id OUTPUT last_rehearsal_at signed_by
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_rehearsal_at,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(last_rehearsal_at) OR days_since>cadence_days
| table bu_id owner last_rehearsal_at days_since cadence_days
new_ucs.description: Moves BCP/DR rehearsal from a 'we did it' statement into a per-business-unit attestation log, mapped to DORA Art.24 and NIS2 Art.21(2)(c).
new_ucs.value: Converts a calendar item into evidence with a signed attestation per rehearsal.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain business_units.csv with owner + cadence; (2) emit bcp_rehearsals.csv after each exercise with a signed_by; (3) run quarterly.
new_ucs.visualization: Heatmap of BU × days-since-rehearsal, table of at-risk BUs, single-value 'BUs within cadence'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: New BUs need an onboarding_date carve-out until the first scheduled rehearsal.

new_ucs.title: Privileged-role specialist training — admins lacking annual deep-training
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT cover general awareness training (covered by 22.46.1); focuses on privileged-role-specific training.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'priv_role_training' (weekly) emits PAM-managed accounts whose owner lacks a current specialist certificate.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Security reminders and periodic training for workforce; extends to privileged roles.
PCI 12.6.3 annual security awareness training must cover role-specific risks.
AT-3 role-based training.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An admin has no specialist training record in the last 365 days; the UC fires the next week.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All admins have a current specialist training record; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: LMS specialist-course completions, PAM vault inventory with owner mapping.
new_ucs.spl:
| rest /services/pam/vault-inventory splunk_server=*
| fields user account_type
| where account_type="privileged"
| join user [search index=lms sourcetype=completion course_id=priv_security_training earliest=-365d | stats max(_time) as last_completion by user]
| where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table user account_type last_completion
new_ucs.description: Provides a named list of privileged-role owners whose specialist training is overdue — a recurring theme in internal audits of ITGC.
new_ucs.value: Makes the 'role-based training' requirement evidence-backed rather than policy-on-paper.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Tag PAM vault entries with the owner; (2) map course completions to users in the LMS; (3) run weekly; (4) auto-enrol overdue owners.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of overdue admins, bar chart of departments, single-value 'admins with current specialist training'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: New privileged users within 30 days of onboarding have a grace window; allow-list via onboarding_date.

new_ucs.title: Tabletop rehearsal evidence — IR plan exercise frequency
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the quality of the exercise (facilitator notes are outside scope).
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'ir_tabletop_frequency' (monthly) emits IR playbooks whose last rehearsal exceeds cadence.

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 incident handling includes regular testing of the IR plan.
DORA Art.24 digital operational-resilience testing.
IR-3 incident response testing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Playbook PB-007 has not been rehearsed in 200 days (cadence: 180 days); the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Playbook rehearsed within 180 days; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: IR playbook register, rehearsal signed attestations.

| lookup ir_rehearsals.csv playbook_id OUTPUT last_rehearsal signed_by
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_rehearsal,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)

| table playbook_id owner last_rehearsal days_since cadence_days

new_ucs.visualization: Heatmap of playbook × days-since-rehearsal, single-value '% of playbooks within cadence'.

new_ucs.title: Developer data-handling training — prod-access engineers lacking training
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT cover all engineering staff — focuses on those with production access to regulated data.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'dev_data_handling_training' (weekly) intersects IdP prod-access groups against LMS DPO-approved course completions.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.32(4) requires awareness of personnel processing personal data.
HIPAA workforce training applies to workforce with ePHI access.
A.6.3 information-security awareness, education, and training.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Engineer bob is in the prod-access group for customer-database but has no current DPO course record; the UC fires the next week.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All prod-access engineers have current training; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: IdP prod-access group membership, LMS completion records for DPO-approved course ids.
new_ucs.spl:
| rest /services/identity/prod-access-groups splunk_server=*
| fields user group scope
| where scope="regulated"
| join user [search index=lms sourcetype=completion course_id=data_handling_v* earliest=-365d | stats max(_time) as last_completion by user]

| table user group scope last_completion
new_ucs.description: Prevents the 'developers with production access but stale training' anti-pattern that surfaces in GDPR Art.32 enforcement cases.
new_ucs.value: Links IdP posture to training posture — the evidence auditors actually ask for.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Tag IdP groups for prod access with regulated scope; (2) publish DPO-approved course IDs; (3) run weekly; (4) auto-revoke access after configured overdue window.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of engineers without training, bar chart by team, single-value 'prod-access engineers with current training'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Freshly-onboarded engineers in the 30-day grace window should be allow-listed by onboarding_date.

new_ucs.title: Control owner attestation freshness
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate individual test outcomes (covered by 22.47.1).
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'owner_attestation_freshness' (weekly) emits controls whose owner attestation is older than cadence.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC1.3 management's commitment to a culture of control includes owner attestation.
CA-2 control assessments and owner attestations.
A.5.35 independent review of information security.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Control AC-2 has an owner attestation from 14 months ago (cadence: 12 months); the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Control attestation within 12 months; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: GRC owner attestation ledger, control_inventory.csv.
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup control_inventory.csv
| lookup owner_attestations.csv control_id OUTPUT last_attested signed_by
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_attested,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where age_days>attestation_cadence_days OR isnull(last_attested)
| table control_id owner last_attested age_days attestation_cadence_days
new_ucs.description: Elevates owner attestation from an email thread to a tracked GRC metric. Owner attestation freshness is a bedrock SOC 2 CC1.3 criterion.
new_ucs.value: Delivers the internal-audit team a continuous view of 'who has signed what'.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Emit owner_attestations.csv from GRC workflow; (2) run weekly.
new_ucs.visualization: Heatmap of control × age buckets, table of overdue controls, single-value '% of controls with fresh attestation'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: New controls in onboarding need a grace window.

new_ucs.title: Evidence-pack drift — auditor-facing vs pre-production evidence
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the content of the evidence itself; only the SHA256 of the compiled pack.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'evidence_pack_drift' (after each publish) checks the published pack SHA256 against the pre-production manifest.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC1.5 accountability for controls includes evidence integrity.
ITGC evidence-integrity requirement.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A pack published to the auditor has a different SHA256 than the pre-production manifest; the UC fires and names the pack.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Hashes match; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: Evidence-pack build pipeline, publication manifest, auditor-delivery signature events.
new_ucs.spl:
index=grc sourcetype=evidence_pack:publish earliest=-30d
| stats latest(sha256) as published_sha latest(pack_id) as pack by _time
| join pack [search index=grc sourcetype=evidence_pack:preprod | stats latest(sha256) as preprod_sha by pack]
| where published_sha!=preprod_sha
| table pack published_sha preprod_sha
new_ucs.description: Compares the pack published to an auditor against the pack that passed internal QA. Divergence is either a reproducibility failure or a tamper event.
new_ucs.value: Protects the integrity of the evidence that underpins the external audit.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Sign both preprod and published packs; (2) run post-publish; (3) on mismatch, alert internal audit and QA pipeline.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of mismatches, single-value 'packs with matching SHA (30d)'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Legitimate last-mile redactions can produce intended SHA changes; capture them in a redaction_log.csv.

new_ucs.title: Continuous control monitoring anomaly — failure-rate trending up
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT replace control deficiency root-cause analysis.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'ccm_failure_trend' (daily) emits controls whose 7-day failure rate exceeds +3σ of the 90-day baseline.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC4.1 ongoing monitoring activities.
CA-7 continuous monitoring; anomaly detection is the operational expression.
A.5.36 compliance with policies, rules, and standards.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Control MFA-PCI-01 has a failure rate of 5% over the last 7 days vs baseline 0.5±0.2%; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Within-band variability; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: GRC continuous-control-monitoring events (pass/fail per execution).
new_ucs.spl:
index=grc sourcetype=ccm:execution earliest=-90d
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count(eval(result="fail")) as fails count as total by _time control_id
| eval fail_rate=fails/total
| eventstats avg(fail_rate) as avg7d by control_id
| stats avg(fail_rate) as baseline stdev(fail_rate) as baseline_sd latest(fail_rate) as current latest(avg7d) as avg7d by control_id
| where avg7d > baseline+3*baseline_sd
| table control_id baseline baseline_sd avg7d current
new_ucs.description: Surfaces controls whose failure rate is trending up — the most reliable early-warning signal that a control is drifting.
new_ucs.value: Gives internal audit a quantitative view of control health rather than a point-in-time pass/fail.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Instrument CCM per control; (2) run daily; (3) open a deep-dive ticket for anomalies.
new_ucs.visualization: Line charts of failure rate with baseline bands, table of controls above threshold, single-value 'controls within baseline'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Controls with naturally bimodal distributions (business-hours only) benefit from a bucketed baseline.

new_ucs.title: Developer-to-production SoD — same developer submits AND approves merge
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT cover intra-sprint experimental branches not deployed to production.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'dev_prod_sod' (hourly) emits merges whose author == approver for production branches.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Direct evidence of SoD deficiency in the deployment pipeline.
CC6.3 logical access authorisation — SoD in code path.
A.8.30 outsourced development and secure development lifecycle.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A merge to main on repo core-app has author==approver; the UC fires within the hour.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Author != approver; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: Git provider audit logs (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket), CI/CD deployment events.
new_ucs.spl:
index=git sourcetype=github:audit OR sourcetype=gitlab:audit OR sourcetype=bitbucket:audit action=merge earliest=-1h
| search target_branch IN (main,master,release*)
| where author=approver
| table _time repo target_branch author approver commit_sha
new_ucs.description: Direct SoD control for merge-to-production: author and approver must be distinct users.
new_ucs.value: Makes a core deployment-pipeline SoD requirement a continuously-enforced technical control.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ensure Git provider captures author + approver; (2) run hourly; (3) on fire, revert merge via pipeline automation.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of violations, bar chart by repo, single-value 'merges SoD-compliant (30d)'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Solo-maintainer repos legitimately have author=approver; these must carry a risk-acceptance annotation.

new_ucs.title: Financial SoD — same identity posts AND approves a journal entry
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT cover system-generated automated postings.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'fin_journal_sod' (hourly) emits GL entries where poster == approver.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Top-priority SOX ITGC finding if journal SoD is not enforced.
SoD in financial system access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A manual journal entry is posted and approved by the same user on the ERP; the UC fires within the hour.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Poster and approver are different users; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: ERP financial-journal audit events (SAP, Oracle E-Business, Workday Financials).
new_ucs.spl:
index=erp sourcetype=sap:gl OR sourcetype=oracle:gl OR sourcetype=workday:gl action=post_and_approve earliest=-1h
| where poster=approver
| table _time entry_id poster approver amount
new_ucs.description: Direct SOX ITGC control: manual journal entries must be posted and approved by distinct identities.
new_ucs.value: Provides continuous evidence of a universally-audited SOX control.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ensure ERP emits both events; (2) run hourly; (3) on fire, escalate to CFO + controller.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of violations, bar chart by business unit, single-value 'SoD-compliant journals (30d)'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: System-generated entries (automated accruals) post under a service account that must not be counted; filter by account type.

new_ucs.title: Vendor-master SoD — same identity creates vendor AND approves payment
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the quality of vendor due diligence.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'vendor_master_sod' (hourly) emits payments whose vendor-creation and payment-approval share the same identity.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Direct SOX ITGC finding pattern — fraud vector.
PCI 7.2.5 access authorisation includes vendor-master control.

controlTest.positiveScenario: User charlie creates vendor Acme Ltd on Monday and approves a $50k payment to Acme on Tuesday; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Vendor creation and payment approval are by distinct identities; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: ERP vendor-master events, AP payment approval events.
new_ucs.spl:
index=erp sourcetype=sap:vendor_master OR sourcetype=oracle:vendor_master action=create earliest=-30d
| stats min(_time) as created_at values(created_by) as creators by vendor_id
| join vendor_id [search index=erp sourcetype=*:ap_payment action=approve earliest=-30d | stats values(approved_by) as approvers by vendor_id]
| eval overlap=mvfilter(match(creators,approvers))
| where mvcount(overlap)>0
| table vendor_id created_at creators approvers overlap
new_ucs.description: Classic fraud-vector control: the ability to create a vendor and approve its payment cannot reside with a single identity.
new_ucs.value: Continuously enforces a control most organisations only sample.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ensure ERP emits both events; (2) run hourly; (3) auto-freeze suspicious vendor payments via AP workflow.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of same-person violations, single-value 'vendors with SoD-compliant lifecycle'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Small-organisation setups with a single AP resource must carry a documented compensating control.

new_ucs.title: Retention policy drift — system config vs policy catalogue
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate execution of retention (covered by 22.49.1).
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'retention_policy_drift' (daily) compares each system's configured retention against the authoritative policy catalogue.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Storage-limitation principle enforced at the system configuration layer.
CPRA data-minimisation and retention disclosure align.
HIPAA retention applies to documentation.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An S3 bucket with data_domain=customer has a lifecycle of 7 years but the policy catalogue requires 3 years; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Bucket lifecycle matches catalogue; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: System retention configuration snapshots, retention_policy.csv.
new_ucs.spl:
index=datastore sourcetype=retention:config earliest=-1d
| lookup retention_policy.csv data_domain OUTPUT policy_days
| eval drift_days=abs(configured_days-policy_days)
| where drift_days>0
| table store data_domain configured_days policy_days drift_days
new_ucs.description: Ensures system-level retention configuration stays aligned to the authoritative policy catalogue.
new_ucs.value: Turns retention policy adherence into a continuously-measured metric per system.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Export retention configuration nightly; (2) maintain retention_policy.csv; (3) run daily.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of drift stores, bar chart of drift magnitude by data-domain, single-value 'stores aligned to policy'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Legal-hold flagged systems legitimately have longer retention; exclude via hold_in_force=true.

new_ucs.title: Cryptographic erasure attestation — per-asset destruction evidence
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate media-destruction workflows; focuses on cryptographic-key destruction as the disposal method.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'crypto_erasure_attestation' (daily) intersects asset-disposal events against KMS key-destruction events.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.17 erasure fulfilled through provable key destruction.
MP-6 media sanitisation with cryptographic erasure.
HIPAA device/media disposal.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An asset destruction event for host host-01 has no matching KMS key-destruction event within 24h; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Matching KMS key-destruction event within 24h; no alert.

new_ucs.dataSources: Asset disposal workflow events, KMS key destruction events.
new_ucs.spl:
index=assets sourcetype=asset:disposal earliest=-7d method=cryptographic_erasure
| join asset_id [search index=kms action=DeleteKey earliest=-8d | fields asset_id key_id _time | rename _time as key_deleted_at]
| eval delta=abs(_time-key_deleted_at)
| where isnull(key_deleted_at) OR delta>86400
| table asset_id key_id _time key_deleted_at delta
new_ucs.description: Correlates asset disposal events with the matching KMS key-destruction events to provide verifiable erasure evidence.
new_ucs.value: Solves the 'how do I prove we actually erased that data?' question that every DPO faces.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Tag asset-disposal events with asset_id and key_id; (2) ingest KMS DeleteKey; (3) run daily.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of orphan disposal events, single-value '% of disposals with matched key destruction'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Shared-key assets require manual validation; exclude via shared_key_assets.csv.
